title: Level 209/offset1
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 209: "Indoor Neighbourhood"}} {{Class Deadzone}} {{Audio|file=Guysimsorryok.mp3|align=left|volume=1}} {{Level ! Theme}} == Description == '''Level 209''' is an extremely deteriorated and hostile version of the former ''"Indoor Neighbourhood"'', now completely dominated by '''The Star-Hearted'''. Unlike the clean and organized appearance previously documented, the entire level appears to be slowly falling apart. The blue wallpaper that once simulated skies still covers large portions of the ceilings and walls, but it is now torn, stained, and peeling away thtoughout many sectors, exposing raw concrete, rusted metallic structures, and massive cracks hidden beneath the artificial decoration. Large portions of the lighting system have completely failed, causing entire corridors to remain in intense red lighting or total darkness.{{Blurimage|caption='''Level 209.'''|file=209wholered.png}} The streets and houses of the level remain, though they now appear entirely abandoned and heavily decayed. Numerous houses have severe structural deterioration, including broken walls, burned furniture, and partially collapsed interiors. Despite the environment’s deteriorated condition, there are a tiny number of sections of the level that remain strangely organized. The silence throughout the level is frequently interrupted by distant metallic sounds originating from far away. These sounds commonly resemble dragging structures, metallic impacts, and construction-like noises occurring somewhere far beyond visible sections of the environment. No confirmed source for these sounds has ever been documented. Many wanderers report constantly feeling observed even in seemingly empty areas. Prolonged stays within the level frequently result in severe paranoia, insomnia, and continuous feelings of discomfort. == The Star-Hearted == '''The Star-Hearted''' no longer maintains the docile and welcoming behavior previously documented. Recent logs describe the entity as extremely hostile, territorial, and unpredictable, treating the entire level as his personal domain. Many older documents describing him as peaceful are now considered outdated or possibly part of a false image made by the entity for a long time. His appearance remains relatively similar, but it now displays severe signs of physical deterioration, like portions of him appearing broken or partially deformed, and the star symbol on his chest now emits a red glow. Despite its hostile behavior, '''The Star-Hearted''' rarely appears impulsive. In many cases, he observes wanderers from a distance for extended periods before silently disappearing. == Fallen Communities == All communities previously documented within '''Level 209''' are currently considered destroyed or missing. Most sectors still display clear signs of past occupation. Barricaded houses, improvised furniture, and abandoned personal belongings remain scattered throughout the streets of the level. In many cases, entire interiors appear to have been left behind during desperate escape attempts, with open doors, functioning lights, and partially arranged tables remaining intact. Human bodies may occasionally be discovered inside houses or near ''The Park''. Most display severe carbonization, extreme deformation, or damage impossible to identify due to their deterioration. Some reports claim certain bodies appear partially fused into nearby walls, floors, or structures. == Entrances & Exits == Most entrances to '''Level 209''' have been blocked by the M.E.G. === Entrances === * Entering certain modern houses found within [[Level Pi|Level 𝝅]], [[Level 626|626]], or the Dead Woods of [[Level 920]] may occasionally lead to deteriorated sections of '''Level 209'''. * ''Entering a specific building in [[Level 11]] that looks like '''Level 209''' on the inside means you have successfully crossed over. '''BLOCKED''''' * ''Finding a rare, fully indoor section covered in bright lights and walls with a blue sky wallpaper in [[Level 9]] will eventually lead you here. '''BLOCKED''''' === Exits === * Remaining near unstable sectors for prolonged periods may cause a wanderer to noclip into [[Level !|another red place...]]
